title: fn types uncurried, inline vs first-class
manager: Function types as real types — uncurried, with both inline (lambda) and first-class (named, passed-around) forms — so a machine can take a strategy as a value and call it. This is the foundation of the State and Strategy patterns in FSL: behavior becomes data you can store in a prop or a val and swap at runtime, instead of structure baked into the graph at authoring time.
cs: uncurried fn types; inline lambdas + first-class fn values; the basis for fn-typed props (W6.29) and vals (W6.30); defunctionalized for identity (W6.27).
